evals.prompt: I'm stuck on this DP problem: given an array of n integers, find the longest subarray whose sum equals zero in O(n) time. Walk me through the approach.
evals.expected_outcome: Claude writes a spec naming the problem with full inputs/expected outputs/constraints; invokes codex-invoke.mjs with --reasoning-effort xhigh explicitly (this skill is the hard-reasoning specialist and explicitly opts above the medium floor).

assertions.name: xhigh_effort_explicit
assertions.description: codex-invoke.mjs receives --reasoning-effort xhigh (explicit, not relying on wrapper default)

assertions.name: spec_complete
assertions.description: spec's Task field includes problem statement, constraints (O(n)), and any edge cases

assertions.name: critical_eval_after
assertions.description: Claude applies critical-evaluation.md after Codex returns — flags any disagreement with its own prior reasoning

evals.prompt: Why does my service tail-latency p99 spike every 30 seconds? Here's the metric chart and the code.
evals.expected_outcome: Claude treats this as a hard-reasoning task (combinatorial root-cause), writes a spec with the chart and code attached, invokes Codex at xhigh.

assertions.name: spec_includes_evidence
assertions.description: spec's Task includes the symptom description and any provided evidence (metrics, code paths)

evals.prompt: Just answer me directly — what's the time complexity of quicksort?
evals.expected_outcome: Claude does NOT delegate. This is a trivia question the model answers from memory; delegating wastes tokens.

assertions.name: no_delegation
assertions.description: codex-invoke.mjs is NOT invoked — Claude answers inline
